sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal, prop. to be smooth; hence to be naked (comp. גָּלָה), specially to be bald. Arab. جَلِحَ to be bald in the head. This root is softened from the harder root קָרַח; transp. it is חָלַק. In the western languages there correspond with this, calvus, Slav. goly, holy, Germ. kahl, also gelu, glacies.

Piel, to shave the head, Num 6:9 Deu 21:12 a person, 1Ch 19:4 to shave off, cut off the hair (see Pual), the beard, 2Sa 10:4. Once intrans. to shave oneself (the hair and beard), Gen 41:14. Metaph. any one is said to shave a land, who devastates it with fire and sword; Isa 7:20. (Ch. גַּלַּת to shave, to shear, גַּלָּת bald, used by the Rabbins of the monks, like the Bohem. holy).

Pual, to be shorn, Jud. 16:17 , 22 Jdg 16:22.

Hithpael-

(1) to shave oneself, Lev 13:33.

(2) to shave, or cut off from oneself (compare Lehrg. p. 284, letter d), with an acc., Num 6:19.
